What we do

Programming support that keeps production moving

Origin CAM Solutions gives machine shops extra programming capacity when the schedule gets tight. We provide clean CNC programs, setup documentation, revisions, and project support so your team can keep jobs moving without pulling your best programmers away from the work that needs them most.

01
CAD Design

We translate your concepts and engineering drawings into production-ready CAD models — from simple prismatic parts to complex freeform surfaces.

02
CNC Programming & Toolpath Optimization

Efficient, verified G-code for 3-Axis machines. We optimize feeds, speeds, and toolpaths to reduce cycle time, extend tool life, and eliminate costly rework.

03
Simulation & Verification

Every program is run through machine simulation before it touches your stock. We catch collisions, gouges, and over-travel — so you don't find them on the shop floor.

04
Setup Sheets & Documentation

Clear, operator-ready setup sheets, tool lists, and work-holding instructions. Your machinists have everything they need to run the first part right.

How we work

From initial design to first article inspection

A disciplined four-step process that eliminates surprises.

STEP 01
Review & Scope

We review your models, drawings, and tolerances. We'll flag any manufacturability concerns before programming begins.

STEP 02
CAD Preparation

Models are prepared for efficient machining — fixture datums defined, reference geometry set, and manufacturing details aligned before programming.

STEP 03
CAM Programming

Toolpaths are generated, feeds and speeds calculated, and the program verified through full machine simulation.

STEP 04
Delivery & Support

Post-processed G-code and setup documentation delivered with direct support through your first article. We're available until the part is in tolerance.

AXIS
Let's work together

Services

What we do,
and how we do it.

A full walkthrough of our process — from the moment you send us a file to the moment your first part is in tolerance.

Full Workflow
Phase 01 — Intake
File review & manufacturability check

You send us your CAD files, drawings, and any relevant specifications. We review everything before a single toolpath is created. We check for features that may be difficult or impossible to machine as drawn, tight tolerances that require special tooling or setups, and missing or ambiguous dimensions that could cause problems downstream.

STEP, IGES, Parasolid accepted Native CAD files accepted DFM report on request NDA signed before file exchange
Phase 02 — Scoping
Quote, timeline, and setup strategy

Once we understand the part, we define the machining strategy: number of setups, fixturing approach, cutting tools required, and estimated cycle time. You get a clear written scope with timeline before we begin programming. No surprises.

Fixed-price quotes available 48hr minimum turnaround on standard parts Rush available for urgent work
Phase 03 — CAD Preparation
Machining geometry preparation

We prepare the model for a smooth programming handoff: establish WCS datums, confirm critical surfaces, define stock geometry, and create fixture reference bodies. This step aligns the CAD, setup, and machining strategy before material or machine time is committed.

WCS & datum definition Stock model creation Critical surface review Fixture body modelling
Phase 04 — CAM Programming
Toolpath generation and optimization

We build the full CAM program: roughing strategies, semi-finishing passes, finish toolpaths, and specialty operations such as drilling, thread milling, and 3-Axis milling work. Feed rates and speeds are calculated for your specific material and tooling. Toolpaths are optimized to minimize air cuts and reduce cycle time — without sacrificing surface quality.

3-Axis Turning & mill-turn Feed & speed optimization Adaptive roughing Surface finishing passes Thread milling & drilling
Phase 05 — Simulation
Full machine simulation and verification

Every program is run through machine-accurate simulation before delivery. We verify for tool-holder collisions, origin limit violations, gouges, and over-travel. You receive a clean, verified program — not one that looks right in the CAM window but fails at the machine.

Machine simulation Collision & gouge detection Origin limit checking Material removal verification
Phase 06 — Delivery
Post-processed G-code and setup documentation

We deliver post-processed G-code configured for your specific machine and controller. Alongside the program, you receive a complete setup package: operator-ready setup sheets with fixturing diagrams, tool lists with cut data, and work-offset callouts. Your machinist has everything needed to load and run confidently.

Controller-specific G-code Setup sheets with diagrams Complete tool lists Work offset documentation
Phase 07 — Support
First-article support and revision

We stay available through your first run. If the machine behaves differently than expected, or an engineering change comes in, we respond quickly. Revisions to the program during first article are included. We consider the job done when your part is in tolerance — not when we hit send on the files.

First-article revisions included Direct access to the programmer Engineering change support

We'll come back with a scope and timeline — usually within one business day.
